title: Due Scheduling, Monotonic Clocks, Backoff, Jitter, and Pause Policy · Language Lens: Feed Go Clock Injection
summary: A ticker polls every feed at one interval, wall-clock jumps duplicate work, failures retry in lockstep, successful publishers never adapt, and paused sources remain due. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go clock injection increment in deterministic adaptive polling scheduler. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Inject wall and monotonic time roles, define inclusive due comparisons, and test forward, backward, and boundary changes.
Persist next attempt, last success, failure streak, publisher hints, pause state, and scheduling reason independently from process memory.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Clock Injection · Theory Feed Go Clock Injection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 3111 during an isolated backup restore exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove persisted due state, injected clocks, adaptive backoff, jitter, pause, and non-overlap; the release claim must bind source, migrations, dependencies, binary, image, configuration, and restored state; then change one schema, constraint, transaction, upsert, migration, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. checks.pattern: // Evidence: feed-go-clock-injection-1utf4r(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?func\s+evidence_feed_go_clock_injection_1utf4r\s*\([^)]*\)\s*\(bool,\s*int,\s*string\)\s*\{(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?scheduleCases < 8)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?dueCases < 3)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?jitterCases < 3)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?overlappingRuns > 0)(?=(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!// Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
checks.description: The increment includes the required executable or reviewable evidence structure.
checks.failureMessage: Complete the full function, prompt contract, or gate record shown in the brief.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using inject and wall plus specific case evidence.
